mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO signals expanding manufacturing output that translates into higher near-term corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, labor market slack reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns after UNRATE exceeds 5.5% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate paired with a simultaneous decline in industrial production signals that restrictive monetary policy is constraining real output, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, declining retail sales signal weakening demand that cannot be offset by nominal pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, declining RSAFS does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate over the preceding three months tightens financial conditions and raises the equity risk premium, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following a 3-month increase in FEDFUNDS are not negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-expected payroll growth signals robust labor income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice near-term earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of months over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate demand and corporate revenue bases, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Quarters with above-median GDPC1 growth do not produce positive 60-day SPX forward returns in at least 55% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1.8 million units signals weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following HOUST readings below 1800 are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.
